text.title: Elasticsearch Index Lifecycle Management and Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in Elastic ILM + index-template docs + production incident postmortems.

rule.statement: An index template (or data-stream template) MUST exist BEFORE the first document is indexed. Default mapping is forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: ES defaults map every string to both `text` and `keyword`, wasting storage + slowing queries. Per-field mappings must be set up-front.

rule.statement: Every time-series index MUST have an ILM policy attached. Manual deletion / rotation jobs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Manual rotation causes accidental data loss or cluster-red. ILM is the only audit-trail-friendly + automatable lifecycle.

rule.statement: Hot phase MUST rollover by `max_primary_shard_size` (e.g. 50 GB) AND `max_age` (e.g. 7d). Date-stamp daily naming without rollover is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Daily indices without rollover produce wildly uneven shard sizes (small days waste shards, big days too large). Rollover gives uniform shards.

rule.statement: Ingest target MUST be the write alias (e.g. `logs-write`), not the underlying index. Rollover swaps the alias atomically; ingest agents must never hardcode the physical name.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded physical index names break on rollover; ingest stops without warning.

rule.statement: Primary shard count per index MUST be 1-3 unless data volume per index > 50 GB. Default 5 shards is forbidden for log workloads.
rule.rationale: Oversharding wastes overhead; >20 shards per node per index degrades search. 1-shard default + rollover is the standard for time-series.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
